jokes.text: But I wasn't injured. Ow! Well, enough governing.
jokes.setup: Sergeant says he wasn't injured to deserve Purple Heart
jokes.punchline: President immediately injures him, then casually moves on
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff with efficient timing. The casual 'well, enough governing' sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: Bendee-Bendee-Boo, what's wrong with you? You're such a lousy mascot
jokes.punchline: Direct insult calling the mascot terrible at his job
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the usually supportive cartoon theme song format.

jokes.text: No one understands a single word you say not even the jerk in the ascot
jokes.punchline: Specific dig at Fred character's pretentious fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific reference that fans will appreciate.

jokes.text: Rite my ring retal rass!
jokes.punchline: Clearly saying 'bite my shiny metal ass' in disguise
jokes.explanation: Perfect fusion of Bender's signature line with Scooby-Doo speech pattern. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: How old are we on a scale of 14 to 32?
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and narrow age range for cartoon characters
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity that highlights the arbitrary nature of cartoon character ages.

jokes.text: Did anyone else hear that weird laugh? It's spooky. It doesn't seem to correspond to anything funny happening.
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall by noting laugh track doesn't match action
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on cartoon conventions and laugh tracks.

jokes.text: I'll say.
jokes.punchline: Completely flat, unhelpful agreement
jokes.explanation: Classic Hermes bureaucratic non-contribution to group discussion.

jokes.text: Rikes! Splinkies! What was that?
jokes.punchline: Made-up exclamations parodying cartoon speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Basic parody humor. 'Splinkies' is mildly creative but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: How about that kabuki theater? Like' where there's a kabuki theater, there's a concession stand.
jokes.setup: Looking for food in the woods
jokes.punchline: Treating kabuki theater like a movie theater with concessions
jokes.explanation: Cultural confusion humor - mixing high and low culture expectations reasonably well.

jokes.text: Say, Fry, why do you always have the munchies? And also your eyes are bloodshot, you're paranoid, and the van has a skunky odor.
jokes.punchline: Listing obvious marijuana use symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good setup of obvious drug references within cartoon parody context.

jokes.text: Search me. No, don't. I'm carrying.
jokes.setup: Common expression 'search me' meaning 'I don't know'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation with immediate panic about contraband
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy. The quick reversal is beautifully executed.

jokes.text: I do enjoy a well-buttered floor.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's bizarre appreciation for the hazard
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg non-sequitur that finds joy in others' misfortune.

jokes.text: George Takei? Reorge Rakei?
jokes.setup: Bendee tries to say George Takei's name
jokes.punchline: Scooby-speech version sounds like Asian stereotype
jokes.explanation: Borderline problematic humor that plays on speech patterns and stereotypes.

jokes.text: Sorry to haunt you so effectively.
jokes.setup: Characters are startled by his appearance
jokes.punchline: Polite apology for being good at scaring people
jokes.explanation: Great character beat that's both polite and absurd, fitting Takei's persona.

jokes.text: Help yourselves. But I warn you, that chicken is over a year old.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions it's dangerously old
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of concerning information presented as hospitality.

jokes.text: How so? This is America, not Japan. Maybe so. I'm not sure.
jokes.setup: Claims basketball insults his Japanese heritage
jokes.punchline: Admits he's not sure if he's even Japanese
jokes.explanation: Absurd character inconsistency that undermines his entire motivation.

jokes.text: Like, none. But thanks for the yucky-tori.
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'yucky' with 'yakitori'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that works but isn't particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Another grave insult to my ancestry.
jokes.punchline: Treats pun as serious cultural offense
jokes.explanation: Reasonable callback to his earlier complaint, showing character consistency.

jokes.text: Cloning is a sin against nature! But yes. Follow me to the professor's cloning room.
jokes.punchline: Immediately offers to help with cloning
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg hypocrisy and willingness to contradict himself for social acceptance.

jokes.text: This is where I keep various lengths of wire.
jokes.punchline: Points out mundane wire storage as if it's impressive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor Farnsworth non-sequitur treating ordinary items as scientific marvels.

jokes.text: What you doin' wearin' an ascot, brother? I think it looks nice.
jokes.punchline: Meek, polite defense of ascot
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that puts the Fred character on the defensive about his fashion.

jokes.text: Hi. This is Larry Bird calling. Listen, my agent sent me that cartoon script, and I've decided I don't want to be involved in any way.
jokes.punchline: Real Larry Bird calls to refuse participation in this very cartoon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke breaking the fourth wall. Actor refusing to be in the show while being in the show is conceptually excellent.

jokes.text: Like, you guys are terrible friends for always sending us to our deaths.
jokes.setup: Group splits up for dangerous mission
jokes.punchline: Points out the obvious unfairness of always being the sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on Scooby-Doo formula where the same characters always get the dangerous job.

jokes.text: Would you do it for a Bendee Brew? Rokay. Ri'm a ralcoholic.
jokes.setup: Offered incentive to do dangerous task
jokes.punchline: Admits to alcoholism in Scooby-speak
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on Scooby Snack formula, replacing innocent treats with addiction.

jokes.text: Yummy, yummy, yummy I've got love in my tummy
jokes.punchline: Sings children's song about love while clearly intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Extended musical bit that runs a bit long but maintains the alcoholism theme.

jokes.text: Never mind. Here, take this.
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic 'oops' and casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the dramatic reveal moment with casual incompetence.

jokes.text: And I would never have gotten away with it, regardless of you meddling kids.
jokes.punchline: Uses 'regardless' instead of 'if it weren't for' - admits inevitable failure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of iconic catchphrase. One word change completely flips the meaning while keeping the rhythm.

jokes.text: But Kabuki theaters don't have a projector. Some do.
jokes.setup: Amy points out logical flaw in explanation
jokes.punchline: Weak, unconvincing defense that some theaters have projectors
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflection that highlights the absurdity of the explanation.

jokes.text: Then, like, why did you do it? I'm mentally ill.
jokes.setup: Asked for real motivation behind scheme
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of mental illness as explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax. Instead of elaborate villain motivation, just honest self-assessment.

jokes.text: And now, introducing the opponents, six Larry Birds!
jokes.setup: Globetrotters practiced against five Larry Birds
jokes.punchline: Actual game has six Larry Birds - they're still unprepared
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes their preparation worthless through simple arithmetic.

jokes.text: Mr. President, our children are fat and dumb, and we blame cartoons.
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of scapegoating cartoons for parenting failures
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with appropriate bluntness about moral panic.

jokes.text: Fine, fine. Get off my lawn. I'll have Agnew put in a call to Hollywood.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive 'get off my lawn' followed by immediate policy capitulation
jokes.explanation: Good Nixon characterization - grumpy but ultimately bending to public pressure.

jokes.text: This will shut up those whining hippies.
jokes.punchline: Admits real motivation is silencing criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing cynical political calculation behind policy.

jokes.text: I must get my hands on those healthy purpleberries. Bort.
jokes.punchline: Inexplicable 'Bort' added to end
jokes.explanation: Establishes recurring nonsensical catchphrase for cereal commercial villain.

jokes.text: It's funny how we never get tired of the word 'purple.' Never.
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment of their own repetitive wordplay
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware moment about the absurd repetition in children's programming.

jokes.text: It's funny how we never get tired of the word 'purple.' Never.
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment of the repetitive wordplay
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary that lampshades the show's own repetitive gimmick effectively.

jokes.text: My sugar-blasted purpleberries are triple-soaked in maple-flavored syrup to start your day with purple power. And the leftover milk looks like Purpleberry Pond.
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly unhealthy ingredients and bizarre visual similarity
jokes.explanation: Standard cereal commercial parody hitting expected beats about sugar content.

jokes.text: Along with juice, toast, ham, eggs, bacon, milk, cheese, liver, waffles and a big horse vitamin.
jokes.setup: Cereal is part of complete breakfast
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly absurd breakfast components ending with horse vitamin
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with nice absurd ending. Horse vitamin is a strong closer.

jokes.text: There goes the purple-hood.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'neighborhood' with color prejudice
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that works on multiple levels - gentrification and color discrimination.

jokes.text: Sorry, cheddar-head.
jokes.setup: Apologizing for prejudice against orange character
jokes.punchline: Uses cheese-based insult for orange character
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that doesn't quite land - the food association feels forced.

jokes.text: Mom, why is there orange in my Purpleberry Puffs? / As an active morn, I care about these things.
jokes.punchline: Mom's generic advertising-speak response about being 'active'
jokes.explanation: Parodies generic cereal commercial dialogue. Familiar target but competently executed.

jokes.text: Two colors? Wow. Bort.
jokes.setup: Discovers cereal now has multiple colors
jokes.punchline: Underwhelmed reaction followed by signature 'Bort'
jokes.explanation: Continuation of running gag but with diminishing returns.

jokes.text: You can really see the sugar. I don't know which I like more, original or type two.
jokes.setup: Child commenting on sugary cereal varieties
jokes.punchline: 'Type two' references diabetes from sugar consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark joke disguised as innocent cereal preference. The medical reference is perfectly disguised.

jokes.text: Buy! Buy! Buy! Bye-bye.
jokes.punchline: Plays on homophones of 'buy' and 'bye'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that works but isn't particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: G.I. Zapp is the code name for an elite group of heavily armed mercenaries, eager to murder the enemy, regardless of the cost in innocent lives.
jokes.punchline: Honest description of military action instead of sanitized version
jokes.explanation: Sharp subversion that exposes the violent reality behind military toy marketing.

jokes.text: Ah! Here we go. No one can complain about this patriotic dreck.
jokes.punchline: Calls his own programming 'dreck' while defending it
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Nixon's cynical view of his own policies.

jokes.text: What's that? A rock? I hate rocks.
jokes.punchline: Declares irrational hatred of geological formations
jokes.explanation: Solid Zapp Brannigan moment showing his petty complaints about everything.

jokes.text: Patriotic peacekeepers who rebuild schools with their bullet-sucking vacuum tanks.
jokes.setup: President editing violent cartoon for children
jokes.punchline: Absurd description of weapons as helpful school equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist reframing of military violence as educational aid. Creative and satirical.

jokes.text: Warning shot. / Excellent plan, Profestro. That'll blow them straight to he... Church.
jokes.setup: Villain planning to fire missile at heroes
jokes.punchline: President editing 'hell' to 'church' mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good censorship humor showing awkward real-time editing. The pause before 'Church' sells it well.

jokes.text: That's no name for a woman. Let's just call her Pat. Nah. Pass.
jokes.punchline: Rejects 'Pat' as too violent, showing extreme overcorrection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of censorship absurdity - even innocent names become problematic.

jokes.text: Incoming surface-to-air... Telegram!
jokes.punchline: President changing to harmless 'telegram'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with anachronistic replacement. Telegram is absurd but maintains rhythm.

jokes.text: Good, because I forgot mine. I'll be home for dinner.
jokes.setup: Pilot asks about parachute, Zapp admits he forgot his
jokes.punchline: President edits crash into dinner plans
jokes.explanation: Good example of absurd censorship turning plane crash into wholesome family time.

jokes.text: At Disneyland. Hi, Tinker Bell.
jokes.punchline: President edits location to Disneyland with character greeting
jokes.explanation: Creative censorship turning war zone into theme park visit with character interaction.

jokes.text: Ready. Aim... Negotiate.
jokes.punchline: President changing 'Fire' to 'Negotiate'
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of famous military command. Single word change completely reverses the meaning brilliantly.

jokes.text: I'm hit. Oh, God, it... Tickles. Oh, no. Freezer Burn is... Sleeping.
jokes.setup: Soldier appears to be fatally wounded
jokes.punchline: President edits death into tickling and sleeping
jokes.explanation: Standard censorship joke turning violence into innocent activities.

jokes.text: I will avenge him, you heartless... Bastards! It's okay if I say it.
jokes.setup: Soldier about to curse on children's TV
jokes.punchline: President breaks fourth wall to defend his own profanity
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment showing Nixon's double standards about what's appropriate.

jokes.text: Now, hold on there, boys. Violence never solved anything. Then how do we decide who gets the ball? There. How do you like your stupid ball now?
jokes.setup: PSA teaches non-violence to resolve conflicts
jokes.punchline: Agnew solves it by destroying the ball violently
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending that completely undermines the anti-violence message with extreme violence.
